joke.raw_joke: I have the heart of a lion, And the eye of a tiger — And a lifetime ban from the zoo

blocks.content:
### seed-335

**MILO_OFFICIAL_SCRIPT_v1**

seed_id: seed-335
edit_level: polish

script:
- setup: "I have the heart of a lion, and the eye of a tiger."
- punchline: "And a lifetime ban from the zoo."
- tag: ~

changes_made: Cleaned the capitalization — the raw had "And" capitalized mid-sentence which broke the reading. Merged the first two lines into a single setup sentence with a comma — the rhythm of the original three-line structure is preserved in delivery (there's a natural beat after "lion" and after "tiger"), but the caption reads as one coherent setup before the punchline drops. The pause before "And a lifetime ban from the zoo" does the work the third line break was trying to do.

caption_notes: Two cards. Setup: "I have the heart of a lion, and the eye of a tiger." Punchline: "And a lifetime ban from the zoo." — The setup must fully commit to the inspirational-quote register before the third beat lands. Do not break the setup into two cards; if the audience sees "heart of a lion" alone on a card, they may read the turn coming too early. Keep the setup as one card so the reveal is clean.

timing_notes: Setup delivered in the cadence of someone reciting an inspirational phrase — steady, mild pride. Natural pause after "lion" in delivery (the comma does this), then "and the eye of a tiger" continues the vibe. 0.5s pause before punchline — this is where the audience is nodding along to the motivational energy. Punchline flat, matter-of-fact — delivered as simply the next item on a list. He is not proud of the ban. He is also not ashamed. It is a fact about his life.

quality_flag: strong

---

### seed-335

**RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1**

**Primary Cast:**

| Role | Cast | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| Narrator / Credential Presenter | Noah Check — HR Rep persona (adapted: Man Presenting His Personal Qualifications) | "I have the heart of a lion and the eye of a tiger" delivered in Noah's flat safety-briefing register sounds exactly like a mission statement on a performance review. His "compliance comedy / rational foil" archetype treats the lion heart and tiger eye as legitimate credentials — items on a professional list. "And a lifetime ban from the zoo" is item three. It receives the same intonation and gravity as items one and two. He does not consider this an unusual thing to add. It is simply also true. |

**Adaptation Notes:**
- Noah: Business casual wardrobe — his standard button-down or polo. No props needed. The joke works entirely in delivery and face. If a setting is needed: a neutral office-adjacent background, or a plain wall — the "presenting credentials" context is established by his posture and delivery rather than props.
- The zoo ban is not explained. Noah does not explain it. He lists it and moves on. His expression after the third item is the same as his expression after the first two: settled, factual, waiting for the next item that is not coming.

**Shot Intent:**
- **Setup:** Noah to camera with mild formal energy — he is presenting information about himself. His posture is straight. His expression is the expression of a man who considers these facts accurate and relevant. He is not reciting an inspirational quote; he is stating biographical data. The first two items ("heart of a lion," "eye of a tiger") are delivered with the same flat cadence as one would say "I have ten years of experience in compliance management."
- **Punchline:** Hold on Noah as he delivers "And a lifetime ban from the zoo." Same cadence. Same expression. He is not embarrassed about the ban. He is not proud of it. It is a condition of his existence that he has accepted and incorporated into his professional profile. He may give a small nod after, as if confirming the list is complete.
